tasks.label: My share of the group project
Teammate's share (just dumped on me)

memory.summary: teammate_A asked me to do their part because they're busy, implying I should just handle everything. That feels unfair since we both need to contribute.
I pushed back, and they immediately agreed. Trust is safe when I set boundaries; no need to apologize for asking.
Teammate_A finally conceded after I set a boundary, proving my stance was right. Still, the missing 'assignment_A' task leaves me confused about what we actually need to do.
I just messaged teammate_A to clarify assignment_A, cutting through the confusion they created earlier. Setting a boundary worked perfectly; now I need them to actually deliver what was promised.
I sent a vague status update and now I need teammate_A to clarify assignment_A immediately. Trust is safe, but the missing task details are driving my stress up.
They finally stopped ghosting me after I set a hard boundary, which feels right. But now the missing assignment details are still driving my stress up.
Still no assignment_A details despite my hard boundaries. It's driving me crazy, but I'm not backing down until they deliver what was promised.
Teammate_A finally responded to my direct demand for specifics, which feels right. Now I just need the actual details before we start working.
Teammate_A finally gave me the assignment details, but now I'm stressed waiting for Professor prof_assignment to confirm the deadline. This feels better than ghosting, but the pressure is still real.
Teammate_A finally replied, which feels good after my hard stance. Now I'm just waiting on the prof's deadline confirmation.
Ugh, no assignment found? That's frustrating. I need to immediately check if the teammate and professor just missed a deadline or forgot to upload it.
Teammate_A actually replied to my message! Finally, the ball is in the professor's court now. Phew, that stress drop feels good.

desires: Get the professor's deadline confirmation ASAP so I don't have to sit here waiting and feeling anxious about our team dynamic.
Finish my specific portion of the project by 19:00 without making any mistakes that could make teammate_A look bad or require me to do extra work tomorrow.
Eat a real meal with cohort_3 tonight before this stress spiral gets too deep, because being alone right now is draining.

goals.description: Send the direct, no-nonsense text to prof_assignment: 'Hey, I need the final deadline for assignment_A confirmed in writing immediately. If you don't send it by tonight, I'm going to assume we're stuck on Monday and I'll just do my own thing instead of waiting around. Don't make me ask again.' Then wait for their reply before touching anything else.
goals.rationale: I can't let this anxiety drag into the evening; I need closure from the professor right now. My assertive side refuses to accept vague answers or implied deadlines without explicit confirmation, and my impulsive nature means I won't sit here worrying about what if they say yes later. I'm setting the boundary that I need an answer NOW.

goals.description: Finish my specific portion of the group project (my_task) by 7 PM tonight. Since the teammate's part was already returned to them, I only need to focus on mine so I don't feel like a doormat but also don't want to burn out with 2 hours of sleep debt.
goals.rationale: The stress is driving me crazy, but since the teammate dropped their work back to me, I technically have nothing left to do except my own part. However, doing that *after* I get the professor's confirmation makes more sense than grinding through it while still anxious about the deadline. Plus, I'm not gonna keep accepting indefinite delays from anyone; once I know the official rules, I'll tackle this.

goals.description: Eat a real meal with cohort_3 tonight before this stress spiral gets too deep, because being alone right now is draining.
goals.rationale: Relationships are way higher priority than task outcomes, but I've got to deal with the immediate deadline panic first. Eating with friends is crucial for my mental state, but I'm too wound up to enjoy it properly until I resolve the professor situation. I'll squeeze this in after I hear back from prof_assignment.

goals.description: Directly confront teammate_A in text to explicitly state that accepting their dumped work is not an option and set a hard boundary for future interactions.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value is high, but my assertiveness axis demands I protect the relationship by setting clear boundaries rather than silently absorbing unfair burdens. Doing this now prevents resentment from festering and maintains my reputation as reliable (not a doormat).

goals.description: Complete my assigned portion of the project immediately after sending the boundary message, focusing only on my own tasks to avoid total burnout before the deadline.
goals.rationale: I need to finish my share to meet the Mon 19:00 deadline without collapsing. While I want to help, my impulsive nature means I can't plan for long-term fairness; I'll just get my part done now and leave the rest for later or refuse it.

goals.description: Post-meal check-in with cohort_3 to vent briefly about the situation and reaffirm our group bond, ensuring no one else knows the specifics yet.
goals.rationale: Maintaining my status as a reliable partner in the close friend group is important, but I don't have the energy right now to manage a public conflict or detailed explanation. I'll handle this casually once I've secured my own needs.

desires: Reassert my boundaries with teammate_A immediately so they know I won't accept their dumping of work without consequence.
Get through this Mon 19:00 deadline without collapsing from the extra burden, even though it feels unfair.
Maintain my reputation as a reliable partner in cohort_3 by not letting this incident spiral into a public conflict today.
